content: The human brain’s active memory consolidation mechanism, where only high-curiosity, unresolved information is selected for long-term storage during off-line replay, can be directly translated to design a more efficient curiosity-driven AI agent memory architecture. Instead of storing all interaction trajectories, the AI agent would only preserve the uncertain, surprising experiences that trigger its intrinsic curiosity module (ICM) for off-line active learning, mirroring how the human brain prioritizes memory encoding based on learning value.
source_topics: memory as active learning human brain mechanisms
